signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical De-escalation + Hawkish Fed Pivot
signals.text: Reports of a nuclear understanding between the US and Iran in Tehran reduce safe-haven demand for JPY while the swearing-in of hawkish Fed Chair Kevin Warsh reinforces the USD yield advantage. The 277bp SOFR-TONA carry spread favors USD upside, and the JPY COT net short of -70,605 contracts suggests speculators are already positioned for this divergence. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 45% hike priced) on 16 June is the primary catalyst for any JPY recovery against this hawkish Fed backdrop.
evidence: Sky News Arabia: Iran nuclear agreement reached

signals.title: GBP/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Monetary Divergence
signals.text: GBP outperformance (+0.587% avg) against AUD weakness (-0.989% avg) creates a 1.576% G8 spread, driven by extreme crowded long positioning in AUD. AUD COT LF long +55,794 (74.8% long) creates significant liquidation risk as the pair closed Friday at 0.7130 amid a broader commodity-linked currency sell-off. The next RBA meeting (mkt: 16% hike priced) on 16 June determines if the current hiking bias can offset this positioning overhang.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Growth Dilemma + Negative Carry
signals.text: EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1606 as the ECB faces a conflict between persistent inflation and weakening eurozone growth, limiting the currency's recovery potential. The 157bp SOFR-ESTR carry advantage for USD and a EUR COT net short of -8,151 contracts maintain downward pressure on the pair. The next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 June is the catalyst for resolving whether growth concerns will force a more dovish stance.

signals.title: NZD/USD — RBNZ Decision + Crowded Retail Longs
signals.text: NZD/USD faces immediate volatility as the RBNZ meeting on 27 May approaches with a 150bp carry disadvantage against the USD. Retail sentiment is extremely stretched at 71% long, creating a contrarian setup for further downside if the RBNZ fails to deliver a hawkish surprise. The NZD COT net short of -19,437 contracts aligns with the broader institutional trend toward NZD weakness.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Oil Volatility + Speculative Short Squeeze
signals.text: USD/CAD closed at 1.3820 as uncertainty regarding Iran negotiations and a hawkish Fed outlook support the Greenback over the Loonie. CAD COT net short of -39,960 contracts (only 28.4% long) leaves the pair vulnerable to a squeeze if WTI oil prices sustain their Friday gains. The next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 10 June will clarify if the 125bp OIS carry disadvantage for CAD will persist.
